easyDashboard.extension.description: Add a project dashboard to VS Code for quick launching of workspaces. Supports remote development projects but does not need to be installed on the remote host.
easyDashboard.view.recent.name: Recently opened projects
easyDashboard.view.projects.name: Projects
easyDashboard.view.activity.name: Activity
easyDashboard.activity.title: Projects
easyDashboard.config.title: Easy Dashboard
easyDashboard.config.showPaths: Show paths on the project entries.
easyDashboard.config.showProjectIcons: Show project icons in the sidebar and dashboard.
easyDashboard.config.alwaysShowViewHeade: Keep Projects view header actions (search, dashboard, …) always visible instead of only on hover.
When enabled, sets `workbench.view.alwaysShowHeaderActions` so the Projects tree toolbar stays visible. This applies to **all** sidebar views (Explorer, etc.), not only Easy Dashboard.
easyDashboard.config.openOnNewWindow: Auto open the dashboard when spawning new windows.
easyDashboard.config.openInNewWindow: Open projects in a new window (current window stays open).
easyDashboard.config.sortMode: Sort order: by name, last opened, or creation date.
easyDashboard.config.folderSizing: Bootstrap CSS classes for how folders scale to your window size.
easyDashboard.config.columnSizing: Bootstrap CSS classes for how columns scale to your window size.
easyDashboard.config.database: List of projects stored in your user config so it can be synced.
easyDashboard.config.projectsRoot: Root folder on disk: the dashboard tree mirrors folders and projects there (local paths only).
easyDashboard.config.viewMode: Default view mode for the dashboard.
easyDashboard.config.chartVisible: Show the activity chart in the dashboard.
easyDashboard.config.uiLocale: Language for Easy Dashboard dialogs, sidebar hints, and dashboard webview. Use this if the editor display language does not apply to the extension (e.g. some Cursor builds).
easyDashboard.config.vercelTeamSlug: Optional Vercel team scope for `vercel link --team`. Use either the team slug (e.g. my-org-slug) or the team ID (e.g. team_xxxxxxxx); both refer to the same team and the CLI accepts either — enter one value only. Leave empty for a personal (non-team) account.
easyDashboard.config.neonOrgId: Optional Neon organization ID (`org_id`) used when a personal API key must be scoped to an organization.
